sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Mithril Protocol continuous development through Intersect on behalf of the vendor.

Recipient: 2026 Treasury Reserve Smart Contract stake address stake1784sdxt6jjennmstphgdu7l7c2scf5d02a6cve2dgn5s2kq5u3j9v, with project-specific management by Intersect and the vendor Teragone identified in the text.

Deliverables: Mithril Protocol Continuous Development, Audit and oversight through Intersect and technically capable third parties, Milestone-based disbursement controls using Intersect treasury management smart contracts

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 3,810,423 ada, consisting of 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for an Intersect Budget Administration fee.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way, enabling rapid state verification without relying on the entire chain.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Mithril is presented as addressing slow full-node synchronization and centralized-trust reliance by light clients.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that funding Mithril through Intersect ensures continuity and long-term sustainability as development transitions from IOG-funded work to a community-governed funding model.

Claim (governance, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states that it achieved the required 67% support threshold during the 2026 Intersect Budget Process Hydra Voting phase and provides voting and audit references.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal states that the requested withdrawal does not breach the applicable 350M Net Change Limit for Epoch 613 to Epoch 713 at the time of submission and links to the governance action for that limit.
missing: Independent evidence for: Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way, enabling rapid state verification without relying on the entire chain.
Independent evidence for: Mithril is presented as addressing slow full-node synchronization and centralized-trust reliance by light clients.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that funding Mithril through Intersect ensures continuity and long-term sustainability as development transitions from IOG-funded work to a community-governed funding model.

Independent evidence for: The proposal states that Teragone has not previously received direct funding from Cardano treasury or community governance programs but has worked on Mithril for several years through IOG-funded collaboration.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 3,810,423 ada, consisting of 3,699,440 ada for Mithril Protocol Continuous Development and 110,983 ada for an Intersect Budget Administration fee." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Mithril uses stake-based threshold multi-signatures to certify Cardano state in a trustless way, enabling rapid state verification without relying on the entire chain." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
